seo.title: Happy Horse 1.0 | Legacy Video Edit Route | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use the legacy Happy Horse 1.0 video-edit route on MaxVideoAI. Use Happy Horse 1.1 for new text, image and reference generations.

overview: Happy Horse 1.0 is the legacy Happy Horse 1.0 video-edit route on MaxVideoAI. Use Happy Horse 1.1 for new text, image and reference generations, and keep 1.0 when a source clip needs video-to-video editing.
pricingNotes: Text, image, and R2V runs use standard per-second rates by resolution. Video edit is billed at a combined input/output rate, so it costs double the standard rate for the selected resolution.

hero.title: Happy Horse 1.0 - legacy video-edit route
hero.intro: Use Happy Horse 1.0 when a selected source clip needs legacy video edit. Start new text, image, and reference generations on Happy Horse 1.1.
hero.badge: Text · Image · R2V · V2V · 720p/1080p · Native audio · Lip-sync

compareLink.label: Compare Happy Horse 1.1 vs Happy Horse 1.0 ->

items.title: Unified text, image, R2V, and V2V production workflows
Video edits with natural-language style direction

technicalOverview.label: Workflows
technicalOverview.body: Happy Horse 1.0 remains available for legacy video edit. Use Happy Horse 1.1 for new text-to-video, image-to-video, and reference-to-video jobs.

technicalOverview.label: Duration
technicalOverview.body: 3-15 s for generation outputs; video edit accepts 3-60 s source clips and caps output to the first 15 s.

technicalOverview.label: R2V references
technicalOverview.body: 1-9 images, addressed as character1 through character9 in the prompt.

technicalOverview.label: V2V edit
technicalOverview.body: One source video, optional reference images up to five, and audio handling set to auto or origin.

technicalOverview.label: Audio
technicalOverview.body: Native synchronized audio and lip-sync are treated as part of the generation.

promptStructure.title: Prompting Happy Horse 1.0
promptStructure.quote: Subject + action + camera + audio cue + workflow-specific references.
promptStructure.description: Keep prompts direct and assign each reference a clear role. In R2V, name each image as character1, character2, and so on.
steps: Choose the workflow: text, image, R2V, or V2V
Describe the subject and visible action
Specify native audio or dialogue cues
Name references explicitly when using R2V or V2V

tips.title: Tips and limits
items: Use T2V for fresh ideas and spokesperson-style native audio shots.
Use I2V when a key visual or first frame is already approved.
Use R2V when identity, wardrobe, product shape, or character continuity matters.
Use V2V when the source clip has the right motion but needs a new look or direction.

prompts.title: Native audio launch clip
prompts.prompt: 16:9 studio product launch clip, a confident creator speaks directly to camera with natural lip-sync, warm key light, subtle camera push, clean room tone and soft launch music.

prompts.title: R2V two-character demo
prompts.prompt: Use character1 and character2 from the reference images in a cinematic two-person product demo, consistent wardrobe, stable faces, natural synchronized dialogue.

prompts.title: Video edit refresh
prompts.prompt: Edit the uploaded clip into a premium launch scene: preserve the person and pacing, refresh the lighting to warm studio gold, keep audio natural with synchronized speech.

faqs.q: What should Happy Horse 1.0 be used for now?
faqs.a: Use Happy Horse 1.0 for the legacy video-edit route. Use Happy Horse 1.1 for new text, image and reference generations.
Yes. Happy Horse is treated as a native-audio model with synchronized speech and lip-sync integrated into the generation flow.
Happy Horse video edit is billed at a combined input/output rate, so V2V is double the standard per-second price for the same resolution.

quickPricingItems: Use 720p for cheaper iteration across text, image, and R2V.
Use 1080p when the shot is ready for review or publication.
Use V2V after the source clip is selected, because video edit has the doubled input/output rate.
custom.relatedSubtitle: Use Happy Horse when native audio, lip-sync, and multiple input workflows matter more than choosing a specialized single-mode model.

relatedItems.title: Seedance 2.0
relatedItems.description: Compare against Seedance when the decision is unified reference control, native audio behavior, and multi-shot generation.

relatedItems.title: Google Veo 3.1
relatedItems.description: Compare against Veo when premium cinematic realism and audio-native output are the main criteria.

custom.finalPara1: Happy Horse 1.0 fits best when an approved source clip needs a directed legacy video edit. Use Happy Horse 1.1 for new text, image and reference generations.
custom.finalPara2: For launch work, start on Happy Horse 1.1, Seedance, Kling or Veo, then return to 1.0 only when a source clip needs the legacy video-edit endpoint.

hero.title: Happy Horse 1.0
hero.subtitle: Legacy video-edit route for source clip adjustments; use Happy Horse 1.1 for new text, image and reference generations.
hero.paragraph: Use Happy Horse 1.0 only when you need the legacy video-edit endpoint. Use Happy Horse 1.1 for new text, image and reference generations.

features.title: Native audio
features.body: Generate dialogue, ambience and SFX with the render when the route supports it.

features.title: Current route moved
features.body: Use Happy Horse 1.1 for new text, image and reference generations.

features.title: R2V references
features.body: Use multiple references to guide identity, motion, style or scene details.

features.title: Video edit
features.body: Modify an existing clip when you need a controlled visual adjustment.

features.title: 720p or 1080p
features.body: Choose the exposed MaxVideoAI resolution before generation.

features.title: Pay-as-you-go
features.body: See exact live price before you generate.

decisionCards.title: Need the current Happy Horse?
decisionCards.body: Start on Happy Horse 1.1 for new text, image and reference generations. Keep 1.0 only for legacy video-edit jobs.

decisionCards.title: Working from references?
decisionCards.body: Assign each file one job: identity, wardrobe, movement, environment or audio mood.

decisionCards.title: Need another audio-native route?
decisionCards.body: Compare with Veo when you are choosing between cinematic Google output and a flexible audio-video workflow.

referenceWorkflows.title: Text-to-video
referenceWorkflows.body: Write the subject, action, camera, style and audio beats in a compact brief.

referenceWorkflows.title: Image-to-video
referenceWorkflows.body: Use a still image to anchor subject, product, wardrobe or composition.

referenceWorkflows.title: R2V references
referenceWorkflows.body: Give each reference one role so identity, movement and environment do not conflict.

referenceWorkflows.title: Video edit
referenceWorkflows.body: Use source video when the job is to change look, pacing or detail without starting over.

referenceWorkflows.title: Audio handling
referenceWorkflows.body: Keep dialogue short and tie SFX to visible actions for cleaner synchronized output.

pricingCopy.title: Happy Horse 1.0 pricing at a glance
pricingCopy.subtitle: Preset native-audio totals - see the exact live price in the app before you generate.
pricingCopy.footnote: All prices are MaxVideoAI display prices in USD credits for preset scenarios.

meta.title: Happy Horse 1.0 Legacy Video Edit Route | MaxVideoAI
meta.description: Use Happy Horse 1.0 as the legacy Alibaba video-edit route on MaxVideoAI. Use Happy Horse 1.1 for new text, image and reference generations.

demo.title: Demo prompt - Happy Horse 1.0
demo.prompt:
Subject: Museum curator in a portrait gallery
Action: Walks through as painted faces come alive
Camera: Smooth dolly through the gallery
Style: Surreal realism, dawn light, marble reflections and soft dust
Audio: Quiet museum ambience, no dialogue
